name: Zerodha Order Router — Retry & Rate Limiter Protection
scenario: Zerodha's order router calls the NSE trading engine. NSE has transient connection failures during market open and imposes a 200-orders/second rate limit. Add @Retry for transient failures and @RateLimiter to self-throttle within NSE's quota.

core.description: application.yml: retry config (maxAttempts=3, 100ms exponential, retryExceptions=IOException, ignoreExceptions=InvalidOrderException). RateLimiter config (200/s, 1s refresh, 200ms timeout).
OrderRouterService.submitOrder(): @RateLimiter (outer) → @CircuitBreaker → @Retry (inner). Three separate fallback methods with appropriate return values.
IdempotentOrderService.submitIdempotent(idempotencyKey, order): @Retry with same idempotency key on all attempts. Result cache prevents double submission.
RetryEventMonitor: counts retry attempts via RetryRegistry event listener. @Scheduled logs stats every minute.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): retry succeeds on 3rd attempt (verify 3 calls), fallback on all-fail, InvalidOrder not retried (verify 1 call), rate limit triggers fallback. All 4 pass.
README documents: annotation stack order rationale, retryExceptions vs ignoreExceptions choices.

bonus.description: RateLimiterDashboard @RestController /admin/rate-limits: returns current rate limiter metrics (available permits, waiting threads). @SpringBootTest MockMvc test.
RetryWithCircuitBreakerComparison: two @SpringBootTest tests proving annotation order matters — wrong order opens CB 3× faster than correct order.
SEBI Order Throttle: second @RateLimiter 'sebi-limit' of 3 orders/second (per-user) combined with the 200/s global NSE limit. Both must pass for order to proceed.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Annotation stack order: @RateLimiter (outermost) > @CircuitBreaker > @Retry (innermost). Each fallback must match the original method's return type and parameters + Exception/RequestNotPermittedException last. ignoreExceptions in application.yml prevents retry on business exceptions (InvalidOrderException). RetryRegistry is a Spring bean — inject like CircuitBreakerRegistry. @BeforeEach: reset CB + clear retry counters between tests.
